epigraph.text: If you tell the truth, you don't have to remember anything.

follyOfTheDay.headline: HOA Fines Resident $5,000 for Painting Fence 'Wrong' Gray
follyOfTheDay.summary:
A Midwestern homeowners association exercised the full destructive capacity of its authority this week by imposing a maximum $5,000 fine on a resident for committing the cardinal sin of aesthetic deviation: selecting Sherwin-Williams 'Storm Cloud' gray for a fence that was supposed to be Benjamin Moore 'Thunder Cloud' gray. To the human eye, these colors are indistinguishable. To the HOA's enforcement committee, this represents a brazen violation of community standards demanding immediate legal retaliation.

The homeowner has filed a counter-suit, though the precise legal theory—arguing that colors cannot be simultaneously distinct yet identical—has yet to be articulated by counsel. The HOA promises to pursue the matter 'vigorously,' a promise that requires no translation: they will spend more money on lawyers than they will ever recover, because principle is a luxury item available only to entities with endless resources and no accountability.

A spokeswoman for the association declined to comment beyond stating that 'standards matter,' a sentiment that rings somewhat hollow when applied to the distinction between two grays that could only be differentiated by a color-blind person's fever dream.
follyOfTheDay.commentary: Democracy in action: three people with laminating machines and access to an attorney's retainer can bend the will of an entire neighborhood. The Founders would be proud.
